rag_rules: Keep component-type contract content on component type owners, and keep implementation-level bindings on component implementation owners.
Place implementation-scope AGREE content only after the subcomponents or connections it references are declared in that owner.
Use the input AADL model as the source of truth for owner names and in-scope structural identifiers.
generic_rules: Fusion should be additive and minimally invasive: preserve existing features, subcomponents, connections, properties, and unrelated text.
Merge or insert annex content only on an owner whose scope makes all referenced identifiers valid.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Preserve the AGREE wrapper exactly as `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Implementation-scope annex content belongs after the declarations it depends on within the same owner.
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantees or other type-scope contract statements on an implementation owner.
Do not place implementation-only bindings or subcomponent-path references on a component type owner.
Do not change architecture declarations or connection structure just to accommodate annex insertion.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
